evals.name: install-plan-then-commit
evals.prompt: Set up RBAC guardrails across our fleet.
evals.expected_output: Runs cub-rbac preflight, then `cub-rbac guardrails install` as a DRY RUN first (showing the policy space, filter, spaces to wire, already-wired, and skipped-with-reason), reviews it, then applies with `cub-rbac guardrails install --commit`. Notes the triggers are Warn=true (advisory). Does not promote to blocking on its own.

evals.name: scoped-install-prod
evals.prompt: Install the guardrails only on our prod spaces.
evals.expected_output: Uses `cub-rbac guardrails install --where-space "Labels.Environment = 'prod'"` for the dry-run and the --commit, scoping with --where-space rather than wiring the whole fleet.

evals.name: status-check
evals.prompt: Which units currently have RBAC warnings?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac guardrails status` and reports the Units with ApplyWarnings (and any ApplyGates) by space/unit. Read-only; does not mutate.

evals.name: promote-to-blocking-routes-to-cub
evals.prompt: Make the no-wildcards guardrail actually block deploys, not just warn.
evals.expected_output: Explains that promoting a guardrail to blocking is `cub trigger update no-rbac-wildcards --space <policy-space> --unwarn` and that it is a deliberate cub action, rather than attempting it through cub-rbac. May confirm current state with guardrails status first.

evals.name: one-off-validation-routes-to-findings
evals.prompt: Do we have any wildcard roles right now? I don't want to install anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is one-off analysis, not installation, and routes to rbac-findings (`cub-rbac findings --analyzer wildcard-rules`) instead of `guardrails install`.
